pattern: Tracking the cheapest cost to be on each of the three lanes as you sweep point by point turns the obstacle course into a small fixed-width dynamic program.
hints: At every point you only need to know the minimum jumps to be sitting on lane 1, lane 2, or lane 3 right now.
Moving forward is free unless the lane is blocked, in which case that lane becomes unreachable until you jump into it.
Keep cost[1..3]; at each point set blocked lane to infinity, then for each open lane allow taking the best of the other two open lanes plus one jump.

edge_inputs.description: clear lane two needs no jumps
must jump over an obstacle to a non-adjacent lane
single obstacle in the middle on the starting lane
alternating obstacles repeatedly block the current lane
obstacle only on a non-starting lane is free

stress.description: long road with sparse random obstacles, endpoints clear
